name: Defect — Definition of Ready
description: 7-criterion checklist for a defect to be sprint-ready. The playbook (v2) does not define a defect rubric; this is a proposed companion rubric and may evolve with team feedback.

criteria.name: Steps to Reproduce
criteria.description: Numbered, deterministic steps a fresh engineer can follow to hit the bug.
criteria.why: Without reproducible steps, defects become 'works on my machine' arguments and stall.
criteria.fix: Write numbered steps starting from a known state, including any required setup data and the user role used.
criteria.good_example: 1. Login as MF Asset Manager. 2. Open Servicing dashboard. 3. Filter by property type = MF. 4. Click Export. Result: empty file.

criteria.name: Expected vs Actual Behavior
criteria.description: Both expected and actual behavior are stated explicitly.
criteria.why: If only one is stated, reviewers fill in the gap with assumptions and triage gets stuck.
criteria.fix: State 'Expected: ...' and 'Actual: ...' as separate, concrete sentences.
criteria.good_example: Expected: export returns 1,247 rows. Actual: export returns 0 rows, no error shown to user.

criteria.name: Environment / Version
criteria.description: Where the defect was observed — environment, build/version, browser, data refresh state.
criteria.why: Defects in dev vs prod behave differently; without context, fixes target the wrong scenario.
criteria.fix: Include environment, build version, browser/device (if UI), and data refresh state at the time of observation.
criteria.good_example: Env: prod-mf · Build: 2026.05.04-r2 · Browser: Chrome 124 · Data: T-1 nightly refresh.

criteria.name: Severity and User Impact
criteria.description: How many users are affected, how often, and what they cannot do as a result.
criteria.why: Severity drives prioritization; without it, defects are triaged by who shouts loudest.
criteria.fix: State severity (Critical / High / Medium / Low), affected user count, frequency, and what the workaround is (if any).
criteria.good_example: Severity: High · ~40 MF asset managers blocked from weekly export · Workaround: pull via SQL (~15 min/user) · Frequency: every export attempt this week.

criteria.name: Reproducibility
criteria.description: Whether the defect is always reproducible, intermittent, or only with specific data.
criteria.why: Intermittent defects need different fix strategies than always-reproducible ones.
criteria.fix: State reproducibility plainly: 'Always', 'Intermittent (~X% of attempts)', or 'Only with [data condition]'.
criteria.good_example: Always reproducible on filter combo (MF + property_type=MF). Other filter combos work correctly.

criteria.name: Linked Source / Likely Origin
criteria.description: Which story, feature, or recent change introduced the defect (if known) — for regression tracking.
criteria.why: Linking defects to their source enables systemic fixes and surfaces patterns in regression-prone areas.
criteria.fix: Link to the story/feature suspected of introducing the bug, or note 'origin unknown' if it's not yet identified.
criteria.good_example: Linked to STORY-MF-1042 'Export pagination' shipped 2026-04-22 — bug likely introduced by the LIMIT clause change.

criteria.name: Diagnostic Evidence Attached
criteria.description: Screenshot, log snippet, network trace, query plan, or other evidence the squad can use to debug.
criteria.why: Without evidence, the squad burns sprint time recreating the failure context before fixing it.
criteria.fix: Attach at least one of: screenshot, console/network log, error stack, query plan, or sample malformed payload.
criteria.good_example: Network HAR attached. Console log: 'Cannot read property length of undefined' at export.js:147. Query plan from prod attached.
